query: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak confirmed case count latest update late June and July 2026. Have any new cases been reported after 21 June 2026 bringing Uganda total above 20? Check ECDC CDTR Week 27, WHO Disease Outbreak News.
answer:
I've completed the research and submitted the structured findings.

**Bottom line:** No new confirmed cases have been reported in Uganda after 21 June 2026. Uganda's total remains at **20 confirmed cases** (including 2 deaths). The total has **not** risen above 20.

This is confirmed directly by the **ECDC** outbreak page (updated 6 July 2026, reflecting 5 July MoH data), which states the last confirmed case was on 21 June with none since. It is corroborated by **WHO DON612** (1,481 global total; 20 from Uganda) and the **WHO DON of 3 July 2026** (20 confirmed cases + 1 probable case who died, as of 2 July). Uganda additionally has 1 probable case (deceased), 16 recoveries, with 15 cases DRC-linked and 5 from local transmission.

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: As of 5 July 2026, a total of 20 confirmed cases, including two deaths, have been reported by the Ministry of Health in Uganda. The last confirmed case was reported on 21 June and no new cases have been reported since then.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda (DON612)
sources.snippet: In total, 1481 confirmed cases; 1460 from the DRC, 20 from Uganda and one from France (linked to DRC); and 454 deaths including two from Uganda, have been reported.

sources.title: Disease Outbreak News: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC & Uganda (3 July 2026)
sources.snippet: As of 2 July, Uganda has reported 20 confirmed cases including two deaths, as well as one probable case who has died.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic
sources.snippet: There have been 506 confirmed deaths in D.R. Congo, and two deaths in Uganda as of the first week of July.

sources.title: Uganda Ministry of Health EVD Daily Dashboard
sources.snippet: Official Uganda MoH daily Ebola Virus Disease reporting dashboard.

sources.title: WHO adds first diagnostic test for Ebola Bundibugyo virus to its Emergency Use Listing
sources.snippet: WHO added the first molecular diagnostic test for Bundibugyo virus (BDBV) to its Emergency Use Listing.

key_facts: Uganda's confirmed case total remains at 20 (including 2 deaths) as of the latest ECDC update (5 July 2026 data, published 6 July 2026).
No new confirmed cases have been reported in Uganda after 21 June 2026 — the last confirmed case was reported on 21 June 2026 (per ECDC).
Uganda's total has NOT risen above 20; the answer to whether new cases pushed it above 20 is NO.
Uganda also has 1 probable case (who died), in addition to the 20 confirmed cases.
Of Uganda's 20 confirmed cases: 15 had travel links to DRC and 5 were associated with local transmission; 16 individuals have recovered.
WHO DON612 reports a global total of 1,481 confirmed cases: 1,460 DRC, 20 Uganda, 1 France; 454 deaths including 2 from Uganda.
WHO DON of 3 July 2026 confirms 'As of 2 July, Uganda has reported 20 confirmed cases including two deaths, as well as one probable case who has died.'
DRC as of 5 July 2026: 1,561 confirmed cases including 506 confirmed deaths (data through 4 July).
Uganda MoH declared the Bundibugyo virus outbreak on 15 May 2026; WHO declared a PHEIC on 16 May 2026.
France reported 1 imported case on 24 June 2026 (humanitarian doctor returning from DRC), linked to the DRC outbreak.

data_points.metric: Uganda cases with DRC travel links
